(sha=6582726791aa314315444fe9aead47913e1ba925)
commands.review_summary:
Adds a lazy hosted ClawHub marketplace feed loader for the official external plugin catalog, with guarded HTTPS fetch, checksum/size/timeout validation, bundled fallback behavior, and tests.

PR surface: Source +371, Tests +256. Total +627 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. for the review finding: source inspection plus a live ClawHub feed read show hosted candidates include sha256:<hex> integrity while PR-head synthesized install metadata returns only npmSpec and defaultChoice. This PR is a new hosted-feed feature, not a current-main bug reproduction.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] Human maintainer review is required because the remaining blocker combines a concrete integrity issue with product/security acceptance of the hosted marketplace trust boundary on a draft, protected-label PR.

target.title: Add hosted external catalog feed loader
target.body: <!-- hosted-feed-stack:start -->## Hosted Marketplace Feed StackThis is PR2 in the hosted marketplace feed stack. The stack should land in order, with maintainer review at each step before the later PRs wire hosted marketplace data into broader user flows.1. #95846: refactor the bundled external plugin catalog toward the feed-shaped data model.2. #95868: add the lazy hosted marketplace feed loader for the configured public feed URL, with guarded HTTPS fetch, checksum, schema, size, timeout, and bundled fallback behavior.3. #95877: add accepted hosted snapshot fallback so a failed or unchanged hosted fetch can reuse the last valid feed payload.4. #95964: persist hosted marketplace feed snapshots in OpenClaw state.5. #95969: add marketplace source profile validation for hosted feed install candidates.6. #95981: add configurable `marketplaces.feeds` and `marketplaces.sources` profiles.7. #96155: add `openclaw plugins marketplace refresh` as the explicit manual snapshot refresh path.8. #96158: add `openclaw plugins marketplace entries` as the explicit manual read path over hosted, snapshot, or bundled fallback entries.9. #96194: add marketplace feed telemetry.The broader follow-up after this stack is to decide whether and how marketplace entries should feed install/search/startup behavior, add automatic conditional refresh using `ETag` / `Last-Modified`, or move to ClawHub producer-side validation for `/v1/feeds/plugins` and `/v1/feeds/skills`.<!-- hosted-feed-stack:end -->## Journey ContextThis is step 2 of the hosted OpenClaw feed path from RFC 19. #95846 made the bundled catalog feed-shaped. This PR adds the guarded hosted fetch path for that same marketplace feed, while leaving the existing synchronous bundled-catalog startup path unchanged.## What This PR Does- Adds an async hosted loader for the configured public marketplace feed URL.- Routes hosted fetches through the existing SSRF guard with HTTPS required and the allowed public feed host constrained.- Sends optional `If-None-Match` and `If-Modified-Since` headers so callers can perform freshness checks.- Reads response bodies with byte and chunk-timeout limits.- Keeps the network guard import lazy so bundled-catalog cold paths do not load the guarded network stack.- Computes `sha256` metadata for the hosted response body.- Validates the hosted document against the feed-shaped official external plugin catalog schema.- Falls back to the bundled catalog on network, status, checksum, size, JSON, or schema failures.`n- Leaves source-specific install integrity mapping out of this transport slice; #95969 owns ClawHub source-profile integrity semantics.## What This PR Intentionally Does Not DoThis PR does not wire the hosted loader into startup, add persistent snapshots, add source-profile selection, interpret `install.candidates[].integrity`, add auth, add signed envelopes, add regional feeds, or change plugin install/search behavior. #95877 adds the loader-level last-known-good snapshot contract on top of this fetch path, and #95969 owns source-profile validation plus ClawHub checksum semantics.## How To ReviewReview this as the hosted transport and validation layer. The important contract is: a hosted marketplace feed can be tried safely, but any unsafe or unusable hosted result falls back to the bundled catalog instead of changing current behavior.## Validation- `pnpm exec oxfmt src/plugins/official-external-plugin-catalog.ts src/plugins/official-external-plugin-catalog.test.ts`- `pnpm exec oxlint --deny-warnings src/plugins/official-external-plugin-catalog.ts src/plugins/official-external-plugin-catalog.test.ts`- `git diff --check`- `CI=1 node scripts/test-projects.mjs src/plugins/official-external-plugin-catalog.test.ts` (25 tests)- `timeout 300s pnpm tsgo:core:test`## Real Behavior ProofBehavior or issue addressed:The hosted catalog fetch path uses the live ClawHub plugin feed by default, accepts the live schema v2 wrapper, normalizes basic marketplace package metadata without interpreting source-specific candidate integrity, and still falls back safely on invalid hosted responses.Real environment tested:Windows checkout `C:\tmp\openclaw-feeds-proof\pr2` at pushed head `2d68751c140f`, source harness via `node --import tsx`, live HTTPS request to `https://clawhub.ai/v1/feeds/plugins`.Exact steps or command run after this patch:1. `node scripts/test-projects.mjs src/plugins/official-external-plugin-catalog.test.ts`2. `.\node_modules\.bin\oxfmt.cmd --check src/plugins/official-external-plugin-catalog.ts src/plugins/official-external-plugin-catalog.test.ts`3. `.\node_modules\.bin\oxlint.cmd --deny-warnings src/plugins/official-external-plugin-catalog.ts src/plugins/official-external-plugin-catalog.test.ts`4. `git diff --check`5. `node --import tsx -e "... loadHostedOfficialExternalPluginCatalogEntries({ snapshotStore: null }) ..."`Evidence after fix:```json{  "head": "6582726791",  "defaultFeedUrl": "https://clawhub.ai/v1/feeds/plugins",  "resultSource": "hosted",  "metadata": { "url": "https://clawhub.ai/v1/feeds/plugins", "status": 200, "checksum": "sha256:90a122c19017ef2c7a0607c8c1cb893ae7871537fe649d39080a52547bed0a7f" },  "entryCount": 59,  "feed": { "id": "clawhub-official", "sequence": 11 },  "firstEntries": [    { "name": "@expediagroup/expedia-openclaw", "id": "@expediagroup/expedia-openclaw", "label": "Expedia Travel", "npmSpec": "@expediagroup/expedia-openclaw@1.0.4" }  ]}```Observed result after fix:The default hosted fetch accepted the live ClawHub feed as `hosted`, returned hosted entries, preserved response checksum/validators, and normalized a marketplace candidate into basic `npmSpec` metadata while deferring source-specific integrity to #95969.What was not tested:Snapshot reuse is not part of this PR. It is covered by the next snapshot-cache PR.
